word_list.word: what does not kill {sb}, makes {sb} stronger
word_list.pronunciation: wˌʌt dʌznˌɑːt kˈɪl ˌɛsbˈiː mˌeɪks ˌɛsbˈiː stɹˈɔŋɡɚ

senses.definition: used to emphasize that facing and overcoming difficult challenges can lead to greater strength and resilience

word_list.word: a burnt child dreads the fire
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ bˈɜːnt tʃˈaɪld dɹˈɛdz ðə fˈaɪɚ

senses.definition: used to imply that negative experiences can leave a lasting impact on a person's behavior, leading them to be cautious and avoid similar situations in the future

word_list.word: smooth seas do not make skillful (sailors|mariners)
word_list.pronunciation: smˈuːð sˈiːz duːnˌɑːt mˌeɪk skˈɪlfəl sˈeɪlɚz mˈæɹɪnɚz

senses.definition: used to imply that experience and skill are developed through facing challenges and adversities, not in easy or peaceful situations

word_list.word: a dose of adversity is often as needful as a dose of medicine
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ dˈoʊs ʌv ædvˈɚsɪɾi ɪz ˈɔfən æz nˈiːdfəl æz ɐ dˈoʊs ʌv mˈɛdəsən

senses.definition: used to imply that facing and overcoming challenges can be beneficial for personal growth

word_list.word: a fault once denied is twice committed
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ fˈɑːlt wˈʌns dɪnˈaɪd ɪz twˈaɪs kəmˈɪɾᵻd

senses.definition: used to suggest that denying a fault or refusing to admit a mistake can lead to repeating the same error

word_list.word: a good example is the best sermon
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɡˈʊd ɛɡzˈæmpəl ɪz ðə bˈɛst sˈɜːmən

senses.definition: used to emphasize that leading by example is a more effective way of influencing others' behavior than simply preaching to them

word_list.word: adversity and loss make a man wise
word_list.phonetic_text: æd.vɜr.sɪ.ti ænd lɔs meɪk ə mæn waɪz
word_list.phonetic_am_text: ād.vēr.si.ti ānd laws meik ē mān vaiz
word_list.pronunciation: ædvˈɚsɪɾi ænd lˈɔs mˌeɪk ɐ mˈæn wˈaɪz

senses.definition: used to imply that going through difficult or challenging situations can provide valuable lessons and insights, ultimately contributing to the development of wisdom and maturity
used to imply that difficult experiences can be valuable opportunities for personal growth and learning

word_list.word: there is no great loss without some gain
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz nˈoʊ ɡɹˈeɪt lˈɔs wɪðˌaʊt sˌʌm ɡˈeɪn

senses.definition: used to highlight the idea that even in times of significant loss or adversity, there is often a hidden opportunity or benefit that can be gained from the experience
